commit.message:
benchmarks: validate native query/eval performance path (#562)

- Rename query_filter → query_simple and use MED_ITERS (20) instead of
  SLOW_ITERS (3) for stable native-path timing
- Add query_and: native AND compound filter benchmark
- Add query_or: native OR compound filter benchmark
- Add eval_expr: df.eval() returning a boolean Series
- Add query_size_1k / query_size_10k size-sweep fixtures (combined with
  query_simple at 100 K these show the native-path overhead trend)
- Update SLOW_ITERS/MED_ITERS comments to reflect query/eval moving to MED tier
- Apply mojo formatter (inline short BenchResult constructor calls)

commit.message:
🚀 Initial commit: AlphaApp — Freeze & Run Linux Apps in Browser

AlphaApp is a middleware platform that freezes Linux VMs after boot
and serves them instantly in the browser via qemu-wasm.

Features:
- 9-step pipeline: publish → build image → boot → freeze → savevm → wasm
- SSE real-time progress streaming (no SignalR)
- Smart .NET project analysis (DLL, port, NuGet → Alpine packages)
- QMP protocol integration with persistent sessions
- .cph package format for portable distribution
- Arabic RTL MVC web interface
- File browser with Termux/proot path detection

Stack: .NET 10, ASP.NET Core MVC, QEMU, Alpine Linux, qemu-wasm

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Feat: add PMS column generalization migration

Add Alembic migration d3e4f5a6b7c8 that renames
Cloudbeds-specific columns to provider-agnostic names:

- listings: cloudbeds_id -> pms_id
- bookings: cloudbeds_booking_id -> pms_booking_id
- rooms: cloudbeds_room_id -> pms_room_id
- oauth_credentials: add pms_type, token_request_count,
  and token_request_window_start columns

Uses batch-mode operations for SQLite compatibility.
Two-pass approach for constraint renames ensures
proper handling by SQLite batch table recreation.
Downgrade raises NotImplementedError.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <claude@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Grimberg <tykeal@bardicgrove.org>

Refactor: generalize PMS column names

Rename Cloudbeds-specific database columns to provider-agnostic
names across models, repositories, API, and services:

- Listing: cloudbeds_id -> pms_id
- Booking: cloudbeds_booking_id -> pms_booking_id
- Room: cloudbeds_room_id -> pms_room_id
- OAuthCredential: add pms_type, token_request_count,
  and token_request_window_start columns
- Update unique constraints to uq_*_pms
- Rename repo methods get_by_cloudbeds_id -> get_by_pms_id
- Update API response fields and Pydantic models
- Update CalendarService UID generation
- Update sync_service and scheduler model references

Co-Authored-By: Claude <claude@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Grimberg <tykeal@bardicgrove.org>

commit.message:
monitoring: replace Promtail with Grafana Alloy; improve Grafana setup

- Replace promtail with Grafana Alloy (alloy.container + alloy.alloy.j2)
  Alloy ships with native systemd journal support in its Docker image;
  Promtail's Docker image lacks libsystemd so journal scraping never worked.
- Add alloy-data.volume for persistent positions
- Remove old promtail quadlet on deploy
- Add git clone task for homelab-monitoring repo (holds provisioning/dashboards)
- Remove redundant grafana-datasources template (provisioning now in repo)
- Update grafana.container: org name, home dashboard, disable telemetry
- Rename journal group comment to reflect Alloy

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

systemd: remove setfacl from podman socket drop-in

SocketGroup=podman + DirectoryMode=0710 + SocketMode=0660 is sufficient
for group members to access the socket. setfacl is not available on all
machines (e.g. Bihar has no acl package installed).

fix(release): rename PyPI package to tqdb (#7)

* fix(release): rename PyPI package to tqdb

turboquantdb was rejected by PyPI (HTTP 400) as too similar to the
existing 'turboquant' package. Rename the PyPI distribution name to
'tqdb' (matches the abbreviation used in all benchmark artefacts).

The Python import namespace stays 'turboquantdb':
  pip install tqdb
  from turboquantdb import Database

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(release): use manylinux_2_28 for aarch64 to fix mimalloc GCC 4.8 failure

The manylinux2014 cross-compile container ships GCC 4.8 which does not
recognise -Wdate-time. libmimalloc-sys passes -Wno-error=date-time to
suppress it, but old GCC rejects unknown -W flags as hard errors.

Fix: use manylinux_2_28 (GCC 12) for aarch64. This properly builds the
aarch64 wheel instead of silently skipping it.

Remove the now-unnecessary continue-on-error workaround.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(release): fix version lookup and docs after tqdb rename

- python/turboquantdb/__init__.py: try 'tqdb' dist name first, fall
  back to 'turboquantdb' (dev/editable installs), then '0.0.0'
- docs/PYTHON_API.md: update install command to pip install tqdb

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: redirects and missing route params for PFE projects (#7418)

* fix: missing URL params for PFE projects

- Pass query params through with redirected URLs, so that links to the new Projects page, from Talk posts, retain their query parameters.
- Add a `redirectPfeToFem` function which redirects any project page that isn't listed in `PFE_SLUGS`, so that PFE project pages can use the dynamic `/projects/:owner/:name` route.
- Use `redirectToStaticProxy` as the catch-all route for both `/*` and `/projects/*`, so that links to the new user group pages, and links to classify specific workflows, subject sets, or subjects work from Talk posts.
- Closes #7023.

commit.message:
feat: TurboQuantDB v0.1.0 — storage engine, ANN index, quantizer, Python API (#4)

* feat: TurboQuantDB v0.1.0 — full feature branch squashed onto main

Squash of feat/engine-docs-ci-enhancements onto current main.
Original branch was rebased after email history rewrite.

All changes from original PR #3 are included:
- Python API: insert_batch, delete_batch, query, list_ids, update_metadata,
  count, __len__, __contains__, include= param, multi-collection support
- Storage: HNSW graph index, WAL, segments, compaction, mmap live codes
- Quantizer: ProdQuantizer (MSE + QJL), codebook, rotation matrices
- Server: Axum HTTP service with RBAC, quotas, async jobs
- Docs: PYTHON_API.md, README cheat-sheet, CHANGELOG 0.1.0
- CI: release workflow, Python smoke tests, benchmark artifacts
- Bug fixes: all PR review comments addressed (13 total)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore(ci): add pre-commit hook with perf regression gate

- scripts/hooks/pre-commit: runs cargo fmt --check, cargo check -q,
  cargo test -q --lib on every commit; runs mini Python benchmark
  (n=500 dim=128 bits=4, 20 queries) when any src/ file is staged
- benchmarks/precommit_perf_check.py: self-contained benchmark;
  thresholds: ingest >=85% baseline, p50 <=120% baseline, recall drop <=3pp
  run with --update-baseline after intentional perf improvements
- benchmarks/perf_baseline.json: committed baseline from current build
- scripts/install_hooks.{sh,ps1}: one-shot installers; use git config
  core.hooksPath so hooks are shared without copying into .git/

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(storage): address all 8 PR#4 Copilot review comments

live_codes.rs — fix len=capacity reopen bug
  LiveCodesFile::open sets len=capacity (file is pre-allocated in
  GROW_SLOTS=16384 increments). On reopen this caused alloc_slot() to
  return slot N+16384 instead of N, skipping 16384 unused slots.
  Add set_len() and call it from engine after loading IdPool so len
  matches actual populated slot count.

metadata.rs — magic mismatch returns Err instead of empty map
  Silent data loss on corrupt/format-changed file replaced by a
  descriptive InvalidData error so callers can fail fast.

compaction.rs — implement full 2-phase compaction protocol
  CompactionPhase::Committed was dead code. Now commit_compaction()
  advances the marker to Committed after old segments are deleted.
  compact_live_records() calls it before returning so a crash between
  deletion and marker removal is safely recoverable (Committed branch
  is a no-op in recovery).

id_pool.rs — insert() returns Result instead of panicking
  assert! for user-controllable conditions (id too long, buffer overflow)
  replaced with proper error returns. All callers in engine.rs updated
  to propagate with ?. Tests updated; new test for oversized id.

bench_batch_crud.rs — fix misleading log labels
  get_many(10k)/delete_many(5k) corrected to get_many(500)/delete_many(250).

bench_search.rs — rename 'Compaction' step to 'Flush WAL'
  Step 2 only calls flush_wal_to_segment(); relabeled to avoid implying
  actual segment compaction/merge happened.

backend.rs — clarify cloud storage error message
  Removed reference to non-existent cloud feature flag; now says
  'not supported yet' to avoid misleading users.

.gitignore / test_bench_tmp — remove local artifact
  test_bench_tmp/turbodb.json removed from tracking; /test_bench_tmp/
  added to .gitignore.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test(storage): fix compaction recovery test to use valid segment

Segment::write_batch now validates the new segment via read_all() before
deleting old segments (crash safety from prior review fix). The test was
writing a 0-byte file which correctly fails validation and keeps old
segments. Use Segment::write_batch with zero records to produce a valid
(8-byte count header) segment so the happy-path recovery deletes old
segments as intended.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: jyunming <hotsaucecing@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
ci: use reusable workflows from IndrajeetPatil/workflows (#1045)

## Summary

Replaces all 16 standalone GitHub Actions workflows with calls to
reusable workflows in
[IndrajeetPatil/workflows](https://github.com/IndrajeetPatil/workflows).

### Changes

All workflow files replaced with lightweight callers that reference
reusable workflows:

| Workflow | Inputs |
|----------|--------|
| `R-CMD-check.yaml` | `error-on: ifelse(...)` |
| `R-CMD-check-hard.yaml` | `extra-packages: any::vdiffr` |
| `check-link-rot.yaml` | — |
| `check-no-warnings.yaml` | — |
| `check-random-test-order.yaml` | — |
| `check-readme.yaml` | `extra-packages: any::ggstatsplot,
any::ggthemes` |
| `check-spelling.yaml` | — |
| `html-5-check.yaml` | — |
| `lint-changed-files.yaml` | — |
| `lint.yaml` | — |
| `pkgdown-no-suggests.yaml` | `pkgdown-source: r-lib/pkgdown` |
| `pkgdown.yaml` | — |
| `pre-commit.yaml` | — |
| `styler.yaml` | — |
| `test-coverage.yaml` | `runner-os: macos-latest` |
| `test-coverage-examples.yaml` | `threshold: 90` |

### Benefits
- Centralized workflow maintenance — changes to CI only need to be made
in one place
- All actions pinned to SHA for supply chain security
- Consistent CI/CD across R package repos
- Upgrades: `lycheeverse/lychee-action` v2→v2.8.0, `actions/cache`
v5→v5.0.4

### Note on `@main` references

The caller workflows reference `IndrajeetPatil/workflows` via `@main`
rather than a pinned SHA. This is intentional — all third-party GitHub
Actions within the reusable workflows are already pinned to specific
commit SHAs (e.g., `actions/checkout@de0fac2...`,
`r-lib/actions@6f6e5bc...`). Since the workflows repository is under the
same owner's control, there is no third-party supply chain risk. This
follows the same pattern used by
[easystats/workflows](https://github.com/easystats/workflows).

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add June 2022 MSE-II papers for Linear Algebra and DAA

- Added Linear Algebra MSE-II June 2022 (20CS401/20IS401/20AM401/20CC401)
  - Linear transformations, Rank-Nullity theorem
  - Gram-Schmidt orthonormalization
  - Least squares solution
  - Probability distribution, Bayes theorem

- Added DAA MSE-II June 2022 (20CS402/20IS402/20AM402/20CC402)
  - Insertion sort analysis
  - Topological sorting, 2-3 trees
  - Horspool algorithm, closed hashing
  - Heap sort, Prim's algorithm
  - Bellman-Ford, Huffman coding

Total: 31 papers across 6 subjects, 29 pages

commit.message:
Fix: address PR #55 review comments

- Add threading.RLock to provider registry (Comment 1)
- Add @provider() decorator for auto-registration (Comment 2)
- Add register_cloudbeds_provider() function (Comment 3)
- Handle None/empty in _parse_date with errors (Comment 4)
- Log and raise on all-property failures in
  get_guest() and get_custom_fields() (Comments 5, 6)
- Update refresh_token() docstring for Cloudbeds (Comment 7)
- Use tuple for rooms/room_ids in DTOs (Comment 9)
- Fix get_guest() docstring re live API calls (Comment 10)
- Add TODO for get_custom_fields() perf (Comment 11)
- Auto-import cloudbeds in providers __init__ (Comment 12)
- Add tests for new behaviors

Co-Authored-By: Claude <claude@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Grimberg <tykeal@bardicgrove.org>

fix: address code review findings

- db_upload.py: guard os.makedirs() against empty dirname when db_path
  has no directory component
- scraper.yml: add explicit permissions block (contents: read) for
  the scrape job to satisfy least-privilege requirement

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: DiegoLopezAroca <144668528+DiegoLopezAroca@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix(ical): deduplicate now() in tests

Capture datetime.now(UTC) once per test to avoid
potential timestamp drift between date assignments.

feat: implement all roadmap items

- Add requirements.txt with pinned dependencies (selenium, pandas,
  openpyxl, pytest, streamlit, python-dotenv, plotly)
- Add argparse CLI arguments and headless Chrome support to all scrapers
  (boescraper.py, boescraper_con_limite.py, boescraper_v2.py,
  multi_scraper.py)
- Fix dividir_enlaces to handle empty link lists
- Add module-level _headless_mode and _output_csv flags to multi_scraper
- Create db_upload.py for SQLite data upload with argparse
- Create dashboard.py Streamlit visualisation dashboard
- Create tests/ with pytest suite (12 tests, all passing)
- Create .github/workflows/scraper.yml for scheduled GitHub Actions runs
- Update README: remove roadmap section, update ToC, project structure,
  and installation instructions to use requirements.txt
